symptoms.symptom_name: Amaurosis fugax (transient painless monocular vision loss, ~10 minutes, full recovery)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient monocular blindness described as a descending curtain is the classic description of amaurosis fugax / retinal TIA.
Platelet-rich microvascular occlusion in a myeloproliferative neoplasm produces transient ischaemic events including amaurosis fugax.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythromelalgia — bilateral burning, throbbing, bright-red warm feet, worse with heat, relieved by cooling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythromelalgia is a hallmark microvascular manifestation of myeloproliferative thrombocytosis, characteristically aspirin-responsive.
Strictly bilateral symmetrical involvement with no fever is not typical of infective cellulitis.
Warm, bright-red, well-perfused bilateral feet are the opposite of the pale, cold, pulseless limb of arterial occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent mild epistaxis over the past week

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Essential thrombocythaemia (acquired von Willebrand / bleeding paradox)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extreme thrombocytosis can cause a paradoxical bleeding tendency, so co-existing thrombosis and mucosal bleeding fit one myeloproliferative process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic dull headache and malaise over two months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific constitutional headache and malaise are common in myeloproliferative neoplasms but not diagnostic alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Foot pain unresponsive to 1 g paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythromelalgia pain is characteristically refractory to simple analgesia and responsive instead to cooling and aspirin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable splenomegaly (2 cm below left costal margin)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Splenomegaly supports an underlying myeloproliferative process — a clinician palpation finding, not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bounding peripheral pulses, brisk capillary refill, no gangrene/ulceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved bounding pulses and brisk capillary refill exclude critical large-vessel arterial occlusion — a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained thrombocytosis on full blood examination (anticipated platelets >450 x10^9/L) with JAK2/CALR mutation

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination + blood film + JAK2 V617F / CALR molecular testing
investigation_details.expected_result: Sustained thrombocytosis (>450 x10^9/L) with a driver mutation (JAK2 V617F or CALR), consistent with essential thrombocythaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The underlying myeloproliferative diagnosis is missed and thrombotic risk goes unmanaged.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The diagnosis is defined by sustained thrombocytosis plus a clonal marker — laboratory and molecular tests unavailable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The co-occurrence of a TIA (amaurosis fugax), erythromelalgia, epistaxis and splenomegaly points to one systemic myeloproliferative driver — but confirmation is laboratory-based and the acute priority is the TIA.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person / ED assessment with full blood examination, blood film and coagulation screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms thrombocytosis and bleeding/clotting status and initiates the TIA stroke-prevention pathway — cannot be obtained via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: JAK2 V617F / CALR / MPL molecular testing (haematology-directed)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establishes the clonal myeloproliferative driver to confirm essential thrombocythaemia and guide cytoreduction decisions.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Neuro-vascular imaging and ophthalmology/neurology review for the TIA
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Amaurosis fugax mandates urgent secondary-prevention work-up to avert a completed stroke or retinal artery occlusion.
The bilateral symmetrical afebrile picture is erythromelalgia, not infection; antibiotics would mislabel a microvascular crisis and delay the correct pathway.
